login.subtitle: a pixel art social world
login.passkey.save_desc: Create a new account secured by your device
login.create_link: Create a free account, no email — or play as guest
login.bunker.nsec_warning: I understand my key will be held in memory. This is less secure than the options above.
login.create.desc: Pick a username and we'll generate your Nostr keys.
login.create.guest_link: or skip and enter as guest
login.create.saved_check: I've saved my private key somewhere safe
login.create.nsec_warning: Your private key (nsec) has been created for you. Save it somewhere safe — it's the only way to log in on another device, and it can never be changed. If you lose it, your account is gone forever.
login.g.combo_desc: Pick a username and a password. The password unlocks this account on any device — choose something you'll remember and don't lose it.
login.g.username_min: Username must be at least 2 characters
login.g.import_link: Already have an account from another app? Recover it
login.g.unlock_import_desc: Enter the password for this account to unlock your key. It's decrypted here in your browser — Google never sees it.
login.g.recover_desc: Pick the backup you made in another Nostr app from your Google Drive. We'll bring that key into Nostr District and unlock it here in your browser — Google never sees it.
login.g.picker_fail: Could not open Google Drive picker
login.g.newid_confirm_desc: This creates a brand-new key on this Google account and abandons your current one. Your old identity and its followers can't be recovered — only do this if your key was compromised. (Your old portable Drive backup will be deleted too.)
login.pw.create_desc: This password unlocks your account on any device. Choose something you'll remember — and don't lose it.
login.pw.enter_desc: Enter the password you set when you created this account.
login.pw.too_short: Password must be at least 6 characters
login.pw.new_desc: You're back in. Choose a new password for next time.
login.fp.title: Forgot your password?
login.fp.desc_faceid: Use Face ID to get back into your account, then set a new password.
login.fp.desc_erase: Your account is encrypted with your password and can't be recovered without it — not by us, not by Google. If you continue, this account is permanently erased and a brand-new one is created in its place.
login.fp.erase_soft: Lost your device too? Erase & start new
login.fid.setup_desc: Add Face ID (or your fingerprint) so you can get back into your account if you ever forget your password. Highly recommended.
login.fid.no_recovery: No Face ID recovery is set up for this account.
login.fid.cancelled: Face ID was cancelled — try again or use your password.
login.fid.unavailable: Face ID recovery isn't available on this device — try your password.
login.fid.setup_unsupported: Face ID isn't supported here — your password still protects your account.
login.fid.setup_fail: Couldn't set up Face ID — your password still protects your account.
login.pizza_day.title: BITCOIN PIZZA DAY
login.pizza_day.aria_label: Bitcoin Pizza Day — click for details
login.pizza_day.p1: On May 22, 2010, programmer Laszlo Hanyecz posted on the BitcoinTalk forum offering 10,000 BTC for two pizzas. Jeremy Sturdivant accepted, ordered two Papa John's pies (~$41 USD at the time), and the first documented real-world Bitcoin transaction was complete.
login.pizza_day.p2: Today those 10,000 BTC would be worth hundreds of millions — a number that shifts every market open. Bitcoiners celebrate the anniversary every May 22 as a reminder of where the currency started and how far it's traveled.
login.pizza_day.extra: Happy {n}th Pizza Day. Stay humble, stack sats, and grab a slice today.
login.whitepaper.title: BITCOIN WHITEPAPER
login.whitepaper.aria_label: Bitcoin Whitepaper Day — click for details
login.whitepaper.p1: On October 31, 2008, an unknown developer using the name Satoshi Nakamoto posted a 9-page paper to the cryptography mailing list: Bitcoin: A Peer-to-Peer Electronic Cash System. It described a money that needed no banks, no governments, and no trusted third parties.
login.whitepaper.p2: Halloween night, months after the global financial crisis. Most readers ignored it. The paper has since reshaped finance, computer science, and the entire concept of money.
login.whitepaper.extra: 🎃 Happy {n}th Whitepaper Day. Satoshi published on Halloween — and vanished like a ghost. 🎃
login.genesis.title: GENESIS BLOCK
login.genesis.aria_label: Bitcoin Genesis Block Day — click for details
login.genesis.p1: On January 3, 2009, Satoshi Nakamoto mined Bitcoin's first block — the Genesis Block. Embedded in its coinbase transaction was the day's Times headline: Chancellor on brink of second bailout for banks.
login.genesis.p2: A timestamp and a manifesto. Block 0 is the only Bitcoin block whose 50 BTC reward is unspendable by design — a permanent monument at the start of the chain.
login.genesis.extra: Happy {n}th Genesis Day. The chain has not stopped since.
login.finney.title: RUNNING BITCOIN
login.finney.sub: Jan 12 · in memory of Hal Finney · {n} years
login.finney.aria_label: Running Bitcoin Day — click for details
login.finney.p1: On January 12, 2009, Hal Finney received the first ever Bitcoin transaction — 10 BTC sent by Satoshi Nakamoto. The day before, Hal tweeted just two words that would echo through history: Running bitcoin.
login.finney.p2: A legendary cryptographer (PGP 2.0 author, RPOW creator), Hal was one of the first people to see Bitcoin's potential. He passed in 2014 from ALS. His final forum post, written from the edge, remains required reading for anyone who works on this thing.
login.finney.extra: Happy {n}th Running Bitcoin Day. We're still running it.
login.nostr_day.title: NOSTR DAY
login.nostr_day.p1: On November 7, 2020, a developer known as fiatjaf published a short spec for Nostr — Notes and Other Stuff Transmitted by Relays. A few event types, a signature, and a network of dumb relays: no company, no algorithm, no off switch.
login.nostr_day.p2: It grew into a censorship-resistant home for notes, zaps, DMs — and worlds like this one. Your keys are your identity, and they travel with you everywhere.
login.nostr_day.extra: 💜 Happy {n}th birthday, Nostr. Post a note, zap a friend.
login.winter.title: WINTER IN THE DISTRICT
login.winter.p1: The district pulls its collar up. Neon hums against the frost, the lake ices over, and the woods go quiet. Snow settles on the rooftops between the relays.
login.winter.p2: Stay warm, stack sats, and keep your keys in cold storage where they belong. The Genesis Block is only days away.
login.valentine.title: LOVE IN THE DISTRICT
login.valentine.aria_label: Valentine's in the District — click for details
login.valentine.p1: For one week the district runs pink. A rose in every jacket pocket, candy hearts on the counters, the neon softening to a warmer glow — Valentine's is the one day the city lets its guard down and admits it has a heart.
login.valentine.p2: Love freely, but guard what matters. The best things are handed to one person and trusted to no one else — a heart, a key, a secret kept close.
login.valentine.extra: 💗 Not your keys, not your sweetheart.
login.july4.title: INDEPENDENCE DAY
login.july4.p1: On July 4, 1776, the thirteen American colonies declared independence from British rule, establishing a nation built on the ideals of individual liberty and self-governance. The Declaration of Independence remains one of the most influential documents in human history.
login.july4.p2: Bitcoin shares something with that founding spirit — a system designed so no single authority can debase, confiscate, or control. Hard money for a free people.
login.july4.extra: Happy {n}th Independence Day. Stay free, stay sovereign.
login.mid_autumn.title: MID-AUTUMN FESTIVAL
login.mid_autumn.sub: Mooncake Festival · under the full moon
login.mid_autumn.p1: On the 15th night of the 8th lunar month, the moon is at its roundest and brightest. For over a thousand years, families have gathered to light lanterns, share mooncakes, and watch the full moon — a festival of reunion, gratitude, and harvest.
login.mid_autumn.p2: The moon looks the same to everyone, everywhere — no borders, no permission needed. Like a network of relays carrying your words to every shore, it belongs to no one and shines for all.
login.mid_autumn.extra: 🏮 Light a lantern, share a mooncake, look up at the same moon.
login.halloween.title: HAPPY HALLOWEEN
login.halloween.p1: On October 31, the veil between worlds grows thin. Costumes, candy, and things that go bump in the night — Halloween is the one night a year when being a little scary is entirely welcome.
login.halloween.p2: In the spirit of decentralization: no single witch, vampire, or ghost controls this network. Stay spooky, stay sovereign.
login.halving.title: BITCOIN HALVING
login.halving.sub: Block reward cuts in half · {n} years since the first
login.halving.p1: Every 210,000 blocks — roughly every four years — Bitcoin's block reward is cut in half. This is the supply schedule baked into the protocol: predictable, transparent, and impossible to change without majority consensus.
login.halving.p2: The halving is Bitcoin's monetary policy in code. No central bank meeting, no surprise rate change. Just a hard cap of 21 million coins approached on a curve that ends sometime around year 2140.
login.halving.extra: Stack hard, mine harder. Difficulty adjusts; conviction shouldn't.
activity_log.title: Activity Log
activity_log.subtitle: Every Nostr event your account has published in the last {n} days, fetched live from relays. Same view any other Nostr client can see.
activity_log.empty: No events found in the last {n} days.

login_info.nostr.title: What is Nostr?
login_info.nostr.body:
The short version

Nostr is a decentralized social protocol. Think of it like email — many independent servers (called relays) carry messages, but no single company owns the network.

Your identity is a key

Instead of an email + password, your Nostr identity is a pair of cryptographic keys:

  • Public key — your username. You can share it freely.
  • Private key (nsec) — your password. Anyone who has this key IS you. Guard it.

The same key works across every Nostr app — chat clients, blogs, wallets, games like this one.

How do I sign in here?

You have a few choices, from easiest to most flexible:

  • New here? Pick 'Create a free account' — we'll generate a key for you.
  • Already have a Nostr key? Use a passkey, browser extension, or remote signer app.
  • Just want to look around? Some clients support guest mode, but Nostr District requires an identity to chat or own anything in the world.

Click the ⓘ next to each option for setup details.

Learn more

nostr.com · nostr.how — friendly intros maintained by the community.
login_info.google.title: Continue with Google
login_info.google.body:
What this does

Sign in with the Google account you already have. We use it to keep an encrypted backup of your Nostr account in your Google Drive, so you can log in from any device.

What Google can & can’t see

  • The app saves one encrypted file in your own Google Drive. You'll see it there — leave it; it's your backup.
  • It's locked with a password you choose (and, optionally, Face ID). Only ciphertext is uploaded — neither we nor Google can read your key.
  • The app only ever touches that one file. It never sees your email, name, contacts, or any other files in your Drive.

Logging back in & recovery

  • On any device: Continue with Google, enter your password, and you’re in.
  • Forgot the password? If you set up Face ID, you can unlock and set a new one.
  • Lose both your password and Face ID and the account can’t be recovered — that is the trade-off for us never holding your key.

Full details are in our privacy policy.
login_info.passkey.title: Login with Passkey
login_info.passkey.body:
What it is

A passkey is a modern login method secured by your device — Touch ID, Face ID, Windows Hello, or your Android biometric. Behind the scenes, we generate a Nostr key for you and lock it inside your device's secure storage.

How it works

  • First time: tap 'Save with Passkey', name your account, and approve the biometric prompt your device shows.
  • Returning: tap 'Continue as [name]' and approve the prompt again. That's it.

Pros

  • No password to memorize or write down.
  • Phishing-resistant — passkeys are tied to this exact domain.
  • Syncs across your devices automatically if you use iCloud Keychain (Apple) or Google Password Manager (Android/Chrome).

Catches

  • Works only on devices that support passkeys. Most modern phones and laptops do.
  • If you lose all your devices and don't have iCloud/Google sync, the account is gone. We can't recover it for you.
  • To use the same account in another Nostr client outside this site, you'll need the underlying private key — see the 'Connect with Nostr' methods for that.

Treat your device's lock screen passcode like a password. Anyone who can unlock your phone can sign in here.
login_info.connect.title: Connect with Nostr
login_info.connect.body:
For existing Nostr users

Already have a Nostr identity from Damus, Amethyst, Primal, Iris, or somewhere else? Use one of these three methods to sign in with that same identity.

Remote Signer (NIP-46)

Most secure. A separate app holds your key and approves each request. Works with Primal Mobile, Amber (Android), and nsec.app (web). Click the ⓘ on that option for setup steps.

Browser Extension

For desktop power users. Install Nostrame or nos2x in your browser, import your key once, and every Nostr site you visit can request signatures with one click.

Private Key (nsec)

The fallback option — paste your raw private key. Less secure because the key sits in browser memory while you're using the app. Only use this if the other options aren't available.

Don't have a Nostr account yet? Hit 'Back' and pick 'Create a free account' instead.
login_info.signer.title: Remote Signer (NIP-46)
login_info.signer.body:
What it is

A separate app holds your private key. When this site needs a signature, it sends a request to that app, and you approve or reject each one — like a hardware wallet for Nostr.

Apps that work

  • Primal Mobile (iOS / Android) — primal.net/downloads
  • Amber (Android only) — github.com/greenart7c3/Amber
  • nsec.app (web) — nsec.app

How to set it up

  1. Install one of the apps above.
  2. Open it. Create or import your Nostr key inside the app.
  3. Back here, click 'Remote Signer (NIP-46)'. We'll show a QR code.
  4. In the signer app, scan the QR code (or paste the bunker:// URL).
  5. Approve the connection in the signer. You're in.

Why it's good

  • Your private key never leaves the signer app.
  • You explicitly approve each post, zap, and DM.
  • Works across every Nostr client that supports NIP-46 — one key, many apps.

Session storage

After your first successful connect, we save a small client-side session key (NOT your nsec) in your browser's localStorage. This lets the signer recognize you on subsequent re-pastes of the same bunker URL, so logout → re-login works without needing fresh approval. Clearing 'cookies and site data' in your browser wipes it.

Catches

  • You'll need the signer app open (or pushable) when you take actions here.
  • Slightly slower than a browser extension — each signature is a round trip to the signer.
login_info.extension.title: Browser Extension
login_info.extension.body:
What it is

A browser plugin that holds your Nostr private key locally and exposes a window.nostr API to any Nostr site. One click signs.

Which one to install

  • Nostrame — open-source, manages multiple Nostr accounts. github.com/getnostrame/nostrame
  • nos2x — minimal, Nostr-only. github.com/fiatjaf/nos2x

How to set it up

  1. Install one of the extensions above from the Chrome/Firefox store.
  2. Open the extension. Create or import your Nostr key.
  3. Reload this page.
  4. Click 'Browser Extension'. The extension will prompt you to allow Nostr District to request signatures.

Why it's good

  • Convenient — every Nostr site you visit in this browser can use the same key.
  • Your private key stays in the extension; the page never sees it directly.

Catches

  • Browser-only. Each browser or device needs the extension installed separately.
  • Mobile browsers usually don't support extensions — use a remote signer instead on phones.
login_info.nsec.title: Private Key (nsec)
login_info.nsec.body:
What it is

Pasting your raw Nostr private key — an nsec1... string — directly into the site. We hold it in memory only for the duration of the session.

Warnings

  • Anyone with your nsec is you. They can post as you, read your DMs, drain your in-game Lightning wallet.
  • The key sits in your browser's memory while the page is open. Any malicious browser extension or compromised script on this site could in theory read it.
  • You should treat your nsec the way you treat your Bitcoin wallet's seed phrase. Never paste it into a site you don't trust.

When to use this

Only when the other options aren't available — you're on a device where you can't install an extension or a signer app, and you don't have a passkey set up. Otherwise, prefer Remote Signer or Browser Extension.

What we do with it

  • Used in memory to sign Nostr events while you're logged in.
  • Never written to disk, never sent to a server.
  • Cleared when you close the tab or log out.

If you've ever pasted your nsec into a site you no longer trust, generate a new key and migrate your follows. There's no 'change password' in Nostr — only key rotation.
login_info.create.title: Create a free account
login_info.create.body:
Brand new to Nostr?

Pick a username and we'll generate a fresh Nostr identity for you. No phone number, no email, no waiting.

The flow

  1. Choose a username (becomes your display name — change it later anytime).
  2. We generate a private key in your browser.
  3. You're shown the key once. Save it.
  4. Check 'I've saved my private key' and enter the world.

Read this carefully

  • The private key is the only way to log in on another device or recover this account.
  • There is no password reset. There is no 'forgot username' recovery. There is no central company that can help you.
  • If you lose the key, the account and everything in it (including your in-game Lightning wallet) is gone.

Where to save it

  • A password manager (1Password, Bitwarden, Apple Keychain, etc.) — easiest, recommended.
  • Written on paper, stored somewhere safe.
  • Not in a screenshot, a cloud notes app, or an email to yourself.

What's next

After you create the account, you can optionally add a passkey so you don't have to paste the nsec every time you log in. Or you can install Nostrame / nos2x and import the key there to use it across other Nostr apps too.
wallet_info.title: About your wallet
wallet_info.body:
How it works

Your wallet is generated automatically from your Nostr private key (nsec). The same nsec always produces the same wallet, so you don't need a separate backup — as long as you keep your Nostr key, you keep your wallet.

It's powered by the Spark protocol, a Lightning-compatible Bitcoin layer-2.

No separate seed phrase

Your wallet is bound to your Nostr key — there's nothing extra to back up. Keep your nsec safe and your wallet is safe. If you lose access to your nsec, you lose access to this wallet.

Self-custodial — with nuance

Your sats are not held by Nostr District. No one in this app can move your funds, freeze your account, or see your balance.

However, this wallet is not the same as cold storage or a hardware wallet. The Spark protocol relies on a network of operators to keep funds liquid and accessible. Your keys stay yours, but day-to-day access assumes the Spark network is online.

Think of it like cash in your pocket — convenient and yours, but not the place for your life savings.

Recommended use

  • Receive and send zaps
  • Buy items in the shop
  • Tip other players
  • Hold small, everyday amounts

For larger amounts, withdraw to a dedicated wallet or cold storage.

Where in-game zaps go

When someone zaps you inside Nostr District, sats land in this in-game wallet — even if you have a different Lightning address on your Nostr profile.

Zaps from other Nostr clients (Damus, Amethyst, Primal, etc.) still go to whatever's set in your Nostr profile (kind:0). To receive external zaps here too, set this wallet as your Lightning address on your profile.

Security

Your wallet is only as secure as your nsec. Anyone with your nsec can decrypt this wallet and spend it. Protect your Nostr key.

  • If you lose your nsec, you lose this wallet — there is no separate recovery phrase to write down.
  • Your wallet seed is encrypted to your own Nostr pubkey (NIP-44) and stored on Nostr relays as a kind:30078 event. Nothing is cached on this device — log in with the same nsec/extension on any browser and the same wallet loads.
  • Only you (or anyone with your nsec / signer) can decrypt the backup. Relays see ciphertext.
  • Treat this as a hot wallet. Fine for in-game amounts, not for savings.

Wallet not loading?

If your balance shows 0 or never finishes loading, the wallet's connection to the Spark network is likely being blocked. Common causes:

  • VPN — many VPNs block the Lightning service or rewrite headers in ways that fail authentication. Try disabling it.
  • Ad blocker / privacy extension — some block lightspark.com by default.
  • Corporate or school network — firewalls often block crypto/Lightning endpoints.
  • Lightspark outage — rare, but possible. Try again in a few minutes.

If you see CORS or 403 errors in the browser console mentioning spark.lightspark.com, this is almost always one of the above — not lost sats. Your balance is safe; the app just can't reach the network to display it.

Learn more

Breez SDK
zap.title: Zap {name}
zap.subtitle: Send a lightning tip
zap.login_required: Log in with a key to send zaps
zap.fee_note: Lightning fees vary by route — usually 0–5 sats. Network fee is added on top of your amount.
ext_warning.title: ⚠ Limited extension support
ext_warning.body:
Your Nostr extension can sign events, but its encryption support is missing or broken. This means:

  • You won't receive direct messages
  • Closed crew chat will appear empty
  • You can't accept join requests or be promoted to admin
  • Crew posts won't decrypt

This is a limitation of the extension itself — it doesn't fully implement NIP-44/NIP-17 encryption — not something Nostr District can fix from this side.

Recommended fix: log out and sign in with your nsec directly, or use a fully-featured extension like Nostrame or nos2x.
tutorial.welcome.title: Welcome to The District
tutorial.welcome.body: A pixel city living on the Nostr protocol. Everything here — chat, rooms, crews — is decentralized and owned by you.
tutorial.moving.title: Moving Around
tutorial.moving.body: Use the Arrow Keys or click anywhere on the ground to walk. You can explore the entire district from the hub.
tutorial.chat.title: Chat
tutorial.chat.body: Press Enter to focus the chat bar and talk with everyone in the district. Type /help to see all available commands.
tutorial.players.title: Other players
tutorial.players.body: Click on any player to open their menu — view their profile, send a DM, zap them sats, visit their room, or trade items with them.
tutorial.buildings.title: Entering Buildings
tutorial.buildings.body: Walk up to a building door and press E or Space to enter. You'll see a prompt appear when you're close enough. Press Esc to leave and return to the district.
tutorial.room.title: Your Room
tutorial.room.body: Head to MY ROOM — it's yours to customize. Change the walls, floors, add furniture, and hang art. Open the terminal with T.
tutorial.hotkeys.title: Panels & Hotkeys
tutorial.hotkeys.body:
G — Crews    M — DMs    F — Follows
S — Settings    B — Polls    T — Terminal
W — Wallet    Tab — World map

Press ? anytime to see all hotkeys. Type /tutorial to reopen this guide.
tutorial.settings.title: Settings
tutorial.settings.body: Press S to open Settings. From there you can browse and apply community Nostr themes, load custom emoji packs, connect an external Lightning wallet via NWC, view your npub / nsec, and pull up the full hotkeys & commands reference.
tutorial.shop.title: The Shop
tutorial.shop.body: Type /shop in chat to open the item shop. Buy clothes, accessories, name colors, animations, and more — paid with Bitcoin over Lightning.
tutorial.bazaar.title: The Bazaar
tutorial.bazaar.body: Type /bazaar to open your item collection. Catch fish, scavenge the district, and find rare items — then trade them with other players or list them for sats. Sales are escrowed and settled over Lightning. Complete sets to unlock auras, and look for the vending machine in the alley.
bz.setdesc.set_eats: Every bite the district has to offer.
bz.setdesc.set_district_royalty: Own every non-seasonal item in the district — all of it, at once. Heavy is the head.
bz.setdesc.set_finney: Collect every Hal Finney tribute item.
bz.setdesc.set_pizza: Collect every Bitcoin Pizza Day item.
bz.setdesc.set_occult: Every charm, card, and cursed thing.
bz.setdesc.set_critters: Every creature that calls the district home.
bz.setdesc.set_bottom_feeder: Collect all junk from the lake.
bz.setdesc.set_night_shift: Fish that only emerge in the dark.
bz.setdesc.set_full_catch: One of every fish in the lake.
bz.setdesc.set_legendary_artifacts: Own every non-seasonal legendary item (no fish, no holidays).
bz.setdesc.set_lore: Artifacts from Bitcoin and Nostr history.
bz.setdesc.set_holiday_vault: Collect one of every holiday item.

bounty.offline: The board is unreadable right now.

bounty.err.full: Too late — all claims are taken.
bounty.err.items: Those items don't match the bounty.
bounty.err.timeout: No response from the oracle — try again.

bz.list_err.no_verify_support: Your Lightning wallet doesn't support payment verification, so items can't sell while you're offline.
bz.list_err.lightning_unreachable: Could not reach your Lightning address — try again.
bz.list_err.not_owned: Couldn't confirm you own this item yet — try again in a moment.

bz.bought_ok: Paid {price} sats — your {item} is on the way!
bz.buy_err.reserved_for_winner: A bid was accepted on this item — it's reserved for the winning bidder. No payment was made.
bz.buy_err.own_listing: That's your own listing — you can't buy it.
bz.buy_err.already_sold: That item was already sold — no payment was made.
bz.buy_err.item_gone: That item is no longer available — no payment was made.
bz.buy_err.not_listed: That item is no longer listed — no payment was made.

tutorial.wallet.title: Your Wallet
tutorial.wallet.body: You have a built-in Lightning wallet — press W to open it. Your Lightning address lets anyone send you sats from any wallet. Spend them in the shop or zap other players.
tutorial.ready.title: You're ready.
tutorial.ready.body:
The district is yours. Explore, meet people, build your room, start a crew.

Everything is on Nostr — your identity travels with you.
